sections.heading: This Directive harmonises the provisions of the Member
sections.summary: This section requires that location data other than traffic data, relating to users or subscribers, can only be processed when made anonymous or with the consent of the users or subscribers, and that the service provider must inform them of the purposes and duration of processing.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates that location data other than traffic data must be anonymised or processed with user consent, which is relevant for assessing data residency and processing in cross-border contexts.

requirements.text: Where location data other than traffic data, relating to users or subscribers of public communications networks or publicly available electronic communications services, can be processed, such data may only be processed when they are made anonymous, or with the consent of the users or subscribers to the extent and for the duration necessary for the provision of a value added service. The service provider must inform the users or subscribers, prior to obtaining their consent, of the type of location data other than traffic data which will be processed, of the purposes and duration of the processing and whether the data will be transmitted to a third party for the purpose of providing the value, added service. Users or subscribers shall be given the possibility to withdraw their consent for the processing of location data other than traffic data at any time.

sections.source_quote: Where location data other than traffic data, relating to users or subscribers of public communications networks or publicly available electronic communications services, can be processed, such data may only be processed when they are made anonymous, or with the consent of the users or subscribers to the extent and for the duration necessary for the provision of a value added service. The service provider must inform the users or subscribers, prior to obtaining their consent, of the type of location data other than traffic data which will be processed, of the purposes and duration of the processing and whether the data will be transmitted to a third party for the purpose of providing the value added service. Users or subscribers shall be given the possibility to withdraw their consent for the processing of location data other than traffic data at any time.

sections.heading: This Directive shall not apply to activities which fall
sections.summary: This section requires that the processing of location data other than traffic data must be restricted to persons acting under the authority of providers of public communications networks and publicly available electronic communications services, and to what is necessary for the purposes of such activities.
sections.dtia_summary: This section limits the processing of location data other than traffic data to necessary purposes and authorised persons, which is relevant for assessing data residency and processing in cross-border contexts.

requirements.text: Processing of location data other than traffic data, in accordance with paragraphs 1 and 2, must be restricted to persons acting under the authority of providers of the public communications networks and publicly available electronic communications services handling billing or traffic management, customer enquiries, fraud detection, marketing electronic communications services or providing a value added service, and must be restricted to what is necessary for the purposes of such activities.

sections.source_quote: Processing of location data other than traffic data, in accordance with paragraphs 1 and 2, must be restricted to persons acting under the authority of providers of the public communications networks and publicly available electronic communications services handling billing or traffic management, customer enquiries, fraud detection, marketing electronic communications services or providing a value added service, and must be restricted to what is necessary for the purposes of such activities.

sections.heading: The service provider must inform the subscriber or user
sections.summary: This section requires the service provider to inform the subscriber or user of the types of traffic data processed and the duration of such processing for the purposes mentioned in paragraphs 2 and 3, prior to obtaining consent.
